B.definition: Having from birth (or as if from birth) a certain quality or character; innate; inherited.

backdrop.definition: A decorated cloth hung at the back of a stage.

backing.definition: To go in the reverse direction.

bail.definition: Security, usually a sum of money, exchanged for the release of an arrested person as a guarantee of that person's appearance for trial.

ballot.definition: Originally, a small ball placed in a container to cast a vote; now, by extension, a piece of paper or card used for this purpose, or some other means used to signify a vote.

bankruptcy.definition: A legally declared or recognized condition of insolvency of a person or organization.

banner.definition: A flag or standard used by a military commander, monarch or nation.

barrel.definition: A round vessel or cask, of greater length than breadth, and bulging in the middle, made of staves bound with hoops, and having flat ends or heads. Sometimes applied to a similar cylindrical container made of metal, usually called a drum.

bass.definition: A low spectrum of sound tones.

battlefield.definition: The area where a land battle is or was fought, which is not necessarily a field.

beam.definition: Any large piece of timber or iron long in proportion to its thickness, and prepared for use.

beast.definition: Any animal other than a human; usually only applied to land vertebrates, especially large or dangerous four-footed ones.

bench.definition: A long seat with or without a back, found for example in parks and schools.

benchmark.definition: A standard by which something is evaluated or measured.

beneficiary.definition: One who benefits or receives an advantage.

betray.definition: To deliver into the hands of an enemy by treachery or fraud, in violation of trust; to give up treacherously or faithlessly.

beverage.definition: A liquid to consume; a drink, such as tea, coffee, liquor, beer, milk, juice, or soft drinks, usually excluding water.

biography.definition: A person's life story, especially one published.

bishop.definition: An overseer of congregations: either any such overseer, generally speaking, or (in Eastern Orthodoxy, Catholicism, Anglicanism, etc.) an official in the church hierarchy (actively or nominally) governing a diocese, supervising the church's priests, deacons, and property in its territory.

bizarre.definition: Strangely unconventional in style or appearance.

blade.definition: The sharp cutting edge of a knife, chisel, or other tool, a razor blade/sword.

bleed.definition: An incident of bleeding, as in haemophilia.

blend.definition: A mixture of two or more things.

bless.definition: To make something holy by religious rite, sanctify.

blessing.definition: To make something holy by religious rite, sanctify.

boast.definition: A brag; ostentatious positive appraisal of oneself.

bonus.definition: Something extra that is good; an added benefit.

boom.definition: A low-pitched, resonant sound, such as of an explosion.

bounce.definition: A change of direction of motion after hitting the ground or an obstacle.

boundary.definition: The dividing line or location between two areas.

bow.definition: A weapon made of a curved piece of wood or other flexible material whose ends are connected by a string, used for shooting arrows.

breach.definition: A gap or opening made by breaking or battering, as in a wall, fortification or levee / embankment; the space between the parts of a solid body rent by violence

breakdown.definition: A failure, particularly mechanical; something that has failed

breakthrough.definition: An advance through and past enemy lines.

breed.definition: All animals or plants of the same species or subspecies.

broadband.definition: A wide band of electromagnetic frequencies

browser.definition: A person or animal who browses.

brutal.definition: Savagely violent, vicious, ruthless, or cruel

buffer.definition: Of the color of buff leather, a brownish yellow.

bureaucracy.definition: Government by bureaus or their administrators or officers.

burst.definition: An act or instance of bursting.

cabinet.definition: A storage closet either separate from, or built into, a wall.

calculation.definition: The act or process of calculating.

canvas.definition: A type of coarse cloth, woven from hemp, useful for making sails and tents or as a surface for paintings.

capability.definition: The power or ability to generate an outcome

capitalism.definition: A socio-economic system based on private ownership of resources or capital.

capitalist.definition: A person who is a supporter of capitalism.

cargo.definition: Freight carried by a ship, aircraft, or motor vehicle.

casino.definition: A public building or room for entertainment, especially gambling.

casualty.definition: Something that happens by chance, especially an unfortunate event; an accident, a disaster.

catalogue.definition: A systematic list of names, books, pictures etc.

cattle.definition: Domesticated bovine animals (cows, bulls, steers etc).

caution.definition: Precept or warning against evil or danger of any kind; exhortation to wariness; advice; injunction; prudence in regard to danger; provident care

cautious.definition: Careful; using or exercising caution; tentative

cemetery.definition: A place where the dead are buried; a graveyard or memorial park.

chamber.definition: A room or set of rooms, particularly:

chaos.definition: The unordered state of matter in classical accounts of cosmogony.

characterise.definition: To depict someone or something a particular way (often negative).

charm.definition: An object, act or words believed to have magic power (usually carries a positive connotation).

charter.definition: A document issued by some authority, creating a public or private institution, and defining its purposes and privileges.

choir.definition: Singing group; group of people who sing together; company of people who are trained to sing together.

chunk.definition: A part of something that has been separated.

circulate.definition: To move in circles or through a circuit

circulation.definition: The act of moving in a circle, or in a course which brings the moving body to the place where its motion began.

citizenship.definition: The status of being a citizen, in its various senses.

civic.definition: Of, relating to, or belonging to a city, a citizen, or citizenship; municipal or civil.

civilian.definition: A person following the pursuits of civil life, especially one who is not an active member of the armed forces.

clarity.definition: The state or measure of being clear, either in appearance, thought or style; lucidity.

clash.definition: A loud sound, like the crashing together of metal objects.

classification.definition: The act of forming into a class or classes; a distribution into groups, as classes, orders, families, etc., according to some common relations or attributes.

cling.definition: Fruit (especially peach) whose flesh adheres strongly to the pit.

clinical.definition: A medical student's session spent in a real-world nursing environment.

closure.definition: An event or occurrence that signifies an ending.

cluster.definition: A group or bunch of several discrete items that are close to each other.

coalition.definition: A temporary group or union of organizations, usually formed for a particular advantage.

coastal.definition: Relating to the coast; on or near the coast, as a coastal town, a coastal breeze

coincide.definition: To occupy exactly the same space.
